meaning: adj.[宗]<<塔木德经>>犹太法典的
examples: Rob did not "understand the difference between Judaism prior to the two Jewish wars in the 1st and 2nd centuries A.D. and later Mishnaic and Talmudic Judaism."
Among Jews in modern Europe, the Talmudic style triumphed and the kabbalah, in the end, vanished.
He learned both Hebrew and Yiddish, and his mother wanted him to be a Talmudic scholar or a rabbi.
Each year another 6, 000-odd haredi yeshiva (Talmudic seminary) students reach the age of 18 and join the ranks of the draft-dodgers.
